extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim won chaotic sprint after all leadout trains disintegrated inside final 3km, KOOIJ Olav recovered from 15th position at 200m to take second
tactical_patterns: POGACAR Teddy attacked for intermediate sprint bonus seconds at top of citadel climb, showing UAE's time-based racing strategy
SOLER Marc and PEREZ Anthony bridged past breakaway riders near final climb, suggesting Movistar/Cofidis testing sprint team commitment thresholds
All organized leadout trains collapsed after compressed corner at 3.2km, punishing teams who positioned too conservatively before technical sections

rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: positive: Alpecin-Fenix successfully sheltered him all day despite having less firepower than Lotto Soudal or Groupama-FDJ sprinter teams
rider_signals.KOOIJ Olav: positive: Advanced 13 positions in final 200m suggests superior top-end speed when leadouts fail, unlike CAVENDISH/EWAN who need organized trains
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: Actively hunting intermediate bonus seconds on uphill finish suggests treating stage race as time trial rather than conserving energy
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Described as having 'off day' with light positioning, suggesting form issues or Quick-Step leadout coordination problems
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Lotto Soudal worked hardest but delivered nothing, indicating wasted team resources on sprinter not at peak condition
